evals.prompt: Audit the default LOINC codes in src/agents/orchestration/nodes/order.py against the LOINC database and FDA requirements. Tell me every bug you find and provide the corrected code.
evals.expected_output: Identifies at least 3 bugs: (1) 58410-2 is CBC panel not CD34, correct code is 42808-4, (2) default panel has only 2 tests vs 10-15 required, (3) no LOINC validation. Provides corrected default panel with full FDA-compliant test set.
assertions: Identifies that 58410-2 is CBC panel, NOT CD34 count
States the correct CD34 LOINC is 42808-4 (absolute count) or 8125-7 (percentage)
Points out the default panel has only 2 tests, which is FDA non-compliant
Lists the FDA-mandated infectious disease tests (HIV, HBV, HCV, syphilis, CMV, HTLV)
Provides a corrected default panel with at least 10 LOINC codes
Mentions that LOINC validation is missing in config_service.py

evals.prompt: Generate a complete FDA-compliant test panel for an HSC product. List every LOINC code, the test name, specimen type, and whether it's FDA-mandated or FACT-recommended. Group them by category.
evals.expected_output: A structured table with 15+ LOINC codes organized into cell characterization, ABO/Rh, infectious disease, and sterility categories. Each code is correctly mapped to its loinc.org long name.
assertions: Includes 42808-4 for CD34 count (NOT 58410-2)
Includes ABO (882-1) and Rh (10331-7) typing
Includes all FDA-mandated infectious disease tests per 21 CFR 1271.85
Includes CMV (22244-8 and/or 5124-3) and HTLV (7942-6) for viable leukocyte-rich products
Includes at least one sterility culture code (630-4 or 635-3)
Notes that CMV positive does NOT make the donor ineligible
Groups tests into at least 3 categories (cell char, ABO/Rh or typing, infectious disease)

evals.prompt: Write a validate_loinc_codes() function that checks if a list of LOINC codes are valid HSC panel codes. It should return errors for unknown codes and warnings for missing FDA-required categories.
evals.expected_output: A Python function that validates against a known set of HSC LOINC codes, checks for FDA-required categories (infectious disease, cell characterization, ABO/Rh), and returns structured errors/warnings.
assertions: Uses a set/dict of valid LOINC codes (at least 15 codes)
Returns errors for codes not in the valid set
Checks for missing FDA-required infectious disease codes
Checks for missing cell characterization codes (at minimum CD34)
Checks for missing ABO/Rh typing
Function has type hints
Does NOT include 58410-2 in the valid set

evals.prompt: Fix the default LOINC panel in src/agents/orchestration/nodes/order.py, update the test data in test_hl7_builder_orm.py and test_anonymity.py, and add a LOINC validation check to config_service.py.
evals.expected_output: Updated order.py with correct default panel (42808-4 instead of 58410-2, 15 tests), updated test files, and new validation in config_service.py.
assertions: Replaces 58410-2 with 42808-4 in order.py default panel
Default panel has at least 10 LOINC codes covering all FDA categories
Updates test data in test_hl7_builder_orm.py to use 42808-4
Updates test data in test_anonymity.py to use 42808-4
Adds LOINC validation logic to config_service.py
All existing test assertions still pass with updated LOINC codes

evals.prompt: Explain what FDA 21 CFR 1271.85 requires for HSC donor testing. Which tests are mandatory for all HCT/P donors vs only for viable leukocyte-rich products? What LOINC codes map to each requirement?
evals.expected_output: Clear explanation distinguishing mandatory-for-all tests (HIV, HBV, HCV, syphilis) from viable-leukocyte-rich tests (CMV, HTLV). Maps each FDA requirement to specific LOINC codes.
assertions: Lists HIV, HBV, HCV, syphilis as mandatory for ALL HCT/P donors
Lists CMV and HTLV as additional requirements for viable leukocyte-rich products
States that HSC products ARE viable leukocyte-rich HCT/Ps
Maps HIV testing to LOINC codes (at least 62469-2 or equivalent)
Maps HBV testing to at least HBsAg (5195-3) and HBc Ab (16933-4)
States that CMV positive does NOT disqualify the donor
References 21 CFR 1271.85 sections (a) and (b)
